candidate_3.raw_text: {"id":"lesson_plan_1","lesson_plan":{"grade_level":"7th Grade","subject":"English Language Arts","duration":"55 minutes","standard":"CCSS.ELA-Literacy.W.7.1 — Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence, including acknowledging alternate or opposing claims and organizing reasoning logically.","objective":"Students will be able to write a clear argumentative paragraph that supports a claim with reasons and evidence, acknowledges an opposing claim, and organizes reasoning logically.","materials":["Whiteboard and markers","Projector or chart paper","Handout: Argumentative Writing Graphic Organizer","Sample argumentative paragraph","Writing paper and pencils"],"lesson_steps":[{"time":"10 minutes","activity":"Introduction to Argumentative Writing","description":"Begin by explaining the purpose of argumentative writing: to convince others by presenting a claim supported with reasons and evidence. Discuss the importance of acknowledging opposing claims to strengthen an argument. Show a simple example on the board."},{"time":"10 minutes","activity":"Analyzing a Sample Argument","description":"Distribute a sample argumentative paragraph. Read it aloud as a class. Identify the claim, reasons, evidence, and the opposing claim within the paragraph. Use a projector or chart paper to highlight these parts."},{"time":"15 minutes","activity":"Guided Practice with Graphic Organizer","description":"Introduce the Argumentative Writing Graphic Organizer. As a class, choose a simple topic (e.g., \"School uniforms should be required\") and fill out the organizer together, listing a claim, reasons, evidence, and an opposing claim with a rebuttal."},{"time":"15 minutes","activity":"Independent Writing","description":"Students write their own argumentative paragraph on a topic of their choice or from a provided list. They must include a clear claim, at least two reasons with evidence, acknowledge an opposing claim, and organize their reasoning logically. Encourage use of the graphic organizer."},{"time":"5 minutes","activity":"Wrap-Up and Sharing","description":"Invite a few students to share their paragraphs. Provide positive feedback focusing on how well they supported their claims and acknowledged opposing views. Summarize key points of the lesson and answer any questions."}]}}
